sections.title: Financial Reporting Standards
sections.content: Financial reporting standards ensure consistency and comparability in financial statements. Key frameworks include IFRS and US GAAP.
keyPoints: IFRS is principles-based, used in 140+ countries
US GAAP is rules-based, used primarily in the US
Key financial statements: Balance Sheet, Income Statement, Cash Flow
Accrual accounting recognizes revenues when earned

practiceQuestions.question: Under accrual accounting, revenue is recognized when:
options: Goods are delivered or services performed
practiceQuestions.explanation: Accrual accounting recognizes revenue when it is earned (goods delivered or services performed), regardless of when cash is received.

practiceQuestions.question: Which of the following is a key difference between IFRS and US GAAP?
options: IFRS is principles-based, US GAAP is rules-based
practiceQuestions.explanation: IFRS uses a principles-based approach providing general guidelines, while US GAAP uses a rules-based approach with specific requirements.

sections.title: Income Statement Analysis
sections.content: The income statement shows a company's financial performance over a period. Key metrics include revenue, gross profit, operating income, and net income.
keyPoints: Revenue - Cost of Goods Sold = Gross Profit
Gross Profit - Operating Expenses = Operating Income
Operating Income - Interest - Taxes = Net Income

formulas.expression: Gross Profit Margin = Gross Profit / Revenue
Net Profit Margin = Net Income / Revenue

practiceQuestions.question: If a company has revenue of $1,000,000 and COGS of $600,000, its gross profit margin is:

sections.title: Balance Sheet Analysis
sections.content: The balance sheet shows a company's financial position at a point in time. It follows the equation: Assets = Liabilities + Equity.
keyPoints: Current assets expected to be converted to cash within one year
Current liabilities due within one year
Working capital = Current assets - Current liabilities

formulas.expression: Current Ratio = Current Assets / Current Liabilities

practiceQuestions.question: A company with current assets of $500,000 and current liabilities of $250,000 has a current ratio of:
